prd.oneLiner: Connect release plans, feature scope, quality gates, approvals, deployments, and communication history.
prd.goal: Raise planned release schedule adherence to 90% or more.
prd.whyNow: Feature scope, test results, approvals, and deployment schedules are spread across tools, making release readiness hard to see.
prd.problem: Feature scope, test results, approvals, and deployment schedules are spread across tools, making release readiness hard to see.
prd.solution: Connect release plans, feature scope, quality gates, approvals, deployments, and communication history.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, spreadsheets, and manual ledgers
prd.differentiator: Manage intake, processing, and operations data as one connected source of truth.

targets.name: Product or engineering employee preparing a release
targets.needs: Quickly register needed work and know its status.
targets.pain: Feature scope, test results, approvals, and deployment schedules are spread across tools, making release readiness hard to see.

targets.name: Release and quality operations coordinator
targets.needs: Manage priority and history and review operations metrics.
targets.pain: Feature scope, test results, approvals, and deployment schedules are spread across tools, making release readiness hard to see.

scenarios.text: Product or engineering employee preparing a release completes release scope, version, and deployment-schedule registration and checks progress.
Release and quality operations coordinator reviews release predictability, defect escape, and deployment-success reporting.

prd.northStar: Share of operations requests completed on time

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Raise planned release schedule adherence to 90% or more.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of completion history against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of registered requests connected to operations reporting

inScope: Release scope, version, and deployment-schedule registration
Release, feature, test, approval, and deployment-status lookup
Release planning, quality gate, approval, deployment, and communication handling
Release predictability, defect escape, and deployment-success reporting
nonGoals: Replacing adjacent systems such as accounting or payroll
assumptions: User and organization data are available through company SSO.
Operators manage policy and classification criteria.
risks: Weak initial classification can cause incorrect processing.
Stale source data can reduce operational trust.
openQuestions: What defines urgent processing and approval?
What are the post-completion verification and reopen rules?
constraints: State, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
